steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the missions' value to science. The key signal: Metzger continues to use Apollo's data today — present tense, active use. The word must capture that the value is still active and has not ended.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'ongoing': Means continuing without interruption. Metzger's present-tense use of the data directly supports this — the missions' scientific value is still in play. ✓
  • Choice B — 'original': Means first or initial. This says the value existed at the start, not that it persists today. Doesn't fit the signal from "continues to use." ✗
  • Choice C — 'displaced': Means replaced or pushed aside. The passage says the data is still being used, the opposite of displaced. ✗
  • Choice D — 'controversial': Means disputed or debated. Nothing in the passage suggests any dispute about the missions' value. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
